title: My life as a Russian exile in Paris: 'France is an improved version of the USSR'
subhead: 'A Russian in Paris' (2/6). After arriving in France in 2022, journalist Elizaveta Osetinskaya settled in Paris' 16th arrondissement. She especially praises the French school system, which her daughter Bella now benefits from.

title: Tariffs: Trump pursues his trade war through every method available
After two diplomats were violently detained in Tehran, France expresses concern for its nationals in Iran
US unveils new tariffs on 60 partners as Trump rebuilds trade agenda
subhead: The levies, effective Friday, range from 10% to 12.5% and impact major economies like China, India and the EU. Earlier this week, the US president also ordered new 50% tariffs on many Canadian products, citing Ottawa's 'discriminatory treatment' of American products including alcohol and automobiles.

title: Bernard Arnault, generous patron of the arts and lover of tax breaks
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (5/6). France's richest man is also one of Europe's leading art collectors. Through the Fondation Louis Vuitton, he combines his passion for the arts with highly lucrative tax arrangements.

title: Bab Al-Mandab Strait threatens to be new front in war with Iran
subhead: The Houthi forces, who announced a blockade of Saudi ports on Monday, claimed responsibility for an attack in the Red Sea targeting two Saudi Arabian oil tankers on Wednesday. Iran and the Houthis are betting that the economic consequences of the war will bring Donald Trump back to the negotiating table.

title: Wildfires in southern France: 'The anxiety comes afterward, when we see the scorched landscape'
subhead: Fires are raging in southwestern France, including a blaze that began on Wednesday, which forced the precautionary evacuation of 20,000 people, destroyed six houses and devastated everything along a stretch of some 10 kilometers.

The mysterious horse-shaped geoglyph England has been maintaining for thousands of years
How Macron narrowly averted his environment minister's resignation
subhead: Monique Barbut, who said she intended to quit the government on Tuesday, July 21, after an agricultural law she contested was adopted, ended up staying on in her role on Wednesday. The twist added a colorful note to the political crisis.

title: Zelensky forced to dismiss Oleksandr Syrsky, Ukraine's armed forces chief
subhead: Yielding to mounting pressure from demonstrators calling for General Syrsky's departure, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky reversed course and has replaced him with General Mykhailo Drapaty.

title: How social media bans for young users, now in effect in France, have spread worldwide
subhead: France has decided to ban those under 15 from major platforms such as Instagram and TikTok. The country is following a global trend that has gained momentum in recent years.

title: The profile of France's new defender of rights raises concern
China's AI doctrine is a challenge for Europe
'Can we continue to entrust global sport to organizations more concerned with their financial interests than with the public good?'
